title: Building a Security Regression Harness
concept: Testing the agent loop against known jailbreaks and injections to verify guardrail effectiveness.

exercises.prompt: What is one of the common security anti-patterns we discussed in the last lesson?
exercises.explain: Direct SQL execution from prompt is a security anti-pattern because it can lead to vulnerabilities.

cards.title: What is a Security Regression Harness?
cards.body: A Security Regression Harness is a set of tests used to check if an AI agent is still safe after changes or updates.

cards.title: Why Test for Guardrails?
cards.body: Testing helps ensure that the agent can't be tricked into performing unsafe actions. It's like checking your car's brakes.

cards.title: Testing Like Building a Wall
cards.body: Think of guardrails as a wall protecting you. A regression harness tests the wall to see if it still stands strong against attempts to break through.

cards.title: Example of a Regression Test
cards.body: By trying to use a known jailbreak string, you check if the AI agent still follows the rules.

cards.title: Myth: Testing is Only for Bugs
cards.body: Testing for security is just as important as fixing bugs because it protects against harmful actions.

cards.title: Visualizing Testing
cards.body: Think of security tests like a detective checking for clues. They look for weaknesses in the system.

left.text: Checks for clues to find weaknesses.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of a Security Regression Harness?

options.text: To make the AI agent more creative

exercises.explain: The main purpose of a Security Regression Harness is to verify guardrail effectiveness by testing the AI agent thoroughly.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A Security Regression Harness is only used when an AI agent is first created.
exercises.explain: A Security Regression Harness is used regularly to ensure safety even after updates or changes.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: The Security Regression Harness tests the AI agent against known ______ to ensure guardrail effectiveness.
exercises.sentence: The Security Regression Harness tests the AI agent against known jailbreaks to ensure guardrail effectiveness.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'jailbreaks' because these tests check for known vulnerabilities.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following scenarios describes a Security Regression Harness effectively?

options.text: Running random input to see what happens.
Deliberately trying known jailbreaks to see if they're blocked.
Only using untested input to check creativity.

exercises.explain: The correct scenario is to deliberately try known jailbreaks. This tests whether the agent's guardrails are effective.

exercises.prompt: What is the main outcome of using a Security Regression Harness?

options.text: It allows the AI agent to execute any command without restrictions.
It strengthens the AI agent's ability to resist harmful inputs.
It improves the AI agent’s efficiency by speeding it up.
It makes the agent less user-friendly by adding extra steps.

exercises.explain: The main outcome is that it strengthens the AI agent's ability to resist harmful inputs, ensuring safety through consistent testing.
